title: Add ownerReference to secrets created for KrknTargetRequest
description:
Similar to tsebastiani-5qn implemented in krkn-operator-acm:

When a secret is created for a KrknTargetRequest in the core operator, add an ownerReference pointing to the KrknTargetRequest CR. This ensures automatic cleanup when the CR is deleted.

Implementation should:
1. Modify secret creation logic to accept the krknRequest parameter
2. Use ctrl.SetControllerReference to set ownerReference
3. Ensure ownerReference is set on both create and update operations
4. Add tests to verify automatic cleanup behavior

Reference implementation: krkn-operator-acm commit 7e4983d

title: Use krknctl MarshalJSON when creating configmap
description:
Similar to tsebastiani-xw5 implemented in krkn-operator-acm:

When creating the configmap schema, use krknctl InputField.MarshalJSON() method instead of manual json.Marshal. This preserves the format expected by the krknctl parser (boolean and required fields as strings).

Implementation should:
1. Update krknctl to v0.10.17-beta or later
2. Replace manual map conversion with InputField.MarshalJSON()
3. Call MarshalJSON() on each InputField individually
4. Use json.RawMessage to collect results before final marshal

Reference implementation: krkn-operator-acm commit befd077

title: Create reusable SyncConfigMapToStore in pkg/configmap
Scenarios API returns 401 with valid admin token
description:
GET /api/v1/scenarios/run returns 401 Unauthorized even with a valid admin JWT token.

Expected: Admin tokens should have access to scenarios endpoints
Actual: Returns 401

Location: Likely authentication middleware or authorization logic

Need to check:
- JWT token validation
- Role-based authorization for scenarios endpoints
- Middleware configuration

comments.author: Tullio Sebastiani
comments.text:
Fixed with two changes:

1. Lazy JWT secret loading in auth middleware (pkg/auth/middleware.go)
   - Added NewLazyMiddleware() to load JWT secret only when first needed
   - Fixes 'cache is not started' error during server initialization
   
2. Enhanced auth middleware logging
   - Added detailed logging for auth failures (missing header, invalid format, token validation errors)
   - Helps debug authentication issues
   
3. Fixed JWT secret AlreadyExists race condition (internal/api/auth_handlers.go)
   - getOrCreateJWTSecret now handles IsAlreadyExists error
   - Retries Get if secret exists but wasn't found initially

Root cause: NewServer() called before controller-runtime cache started, causing getOrCreateJWTSecret() to fail when accessing Kubernetes API.

Solution: Lazy load JWT secret on first authenticated request instead of during server initialization.

title: Add User CRUD endpoints to API router
description:
Register all new user management endpoints in the API server.

Add to internal/api/server.go in NewServer():

// User management endpoints - admin only for most, some allow self-access
mux.Handle("/api/v1/users", authMw.RequireAuth(http.HandlerFunc(handler.ListUsers)))
mux.Handle("/api/v1/users/", authMw.RequireAuth(http.HandlerFunc(handler.UsersRouter)))

Create UsersRouter in user_handlers.go to route by method and path:
- GET /api/v1/users -> ListUsers (admin only)
- POST /api/v1/users -> CreateUser (admin only)
- GET /api/v1/users/:userId -> GetUser (admin or self)
- PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId -> UpdateUser (admin or self with restrictions)
- DELETE /api/v1/users/:userId -> DeleteUser (admin only)
- PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId/password -> ChangePassword (admin or self)

Similar pattern to existing routers:
- TargetsHandler
- ProvidersRouter
- TargetsCRUDRouter

Implementation notes:
- Extract userId from URL path
- Route based on HTTP method
- All endpoints require authentication (authMw.RequireAuth)
- Individual handlers check admin vs user permissions internally
- Return 405 Method Not Allowed for unsupported methods
- Return 404 for unknown paths

Dependencies:
- Requires tsebastiani-7rn (ListUsers)
- Requires tsebastiani-how (GetUser)
- Requires tsebastiani-6t8 (CreateUser)
- Requires tsebastiani-qa2 (UpdateUser)
- Requires tsebastiani-dte (DeleteUser)
- Requires tsebastiani-42i (ChangePassword)

Files to modify:
- internal/api/server.go

Files to create:
- internal/api/user_handlers.go

title: Register fails with 'secret already exists' error
description:
When trying to register a user that was partially created before (secret exists but KrknUser doesn't), registration fails with:
  
Error: secrets "krknuser-password-xxx" already exists

This happens because a previous registration attempt created the secret but failed to create the KrknUser.

Location: internal/api/auth_handlers.go:230

Fix: Handle 'already exists' error for secrets:
1. Check if secret already exists before creating
2. If it exists and KrknUser doesn't, delete old secret and create new one
3. If both exist, return proper 'user already exists' error

title: Backend: Implement User CRUD API endpoints
description:
The frontend console needs full CRUD API endpoints for user management.

Currently available in backend:
- POST /api/v1/auth/register - Register first admin
- POST /api/v1/auth/login - Login and get JWT token
- GET /api/v1/auth/is-registered - Check if admin exists

REQUIRED new endpoints:
- GET /api/v1/users - List all users (admin only)
  - Query params: role, active, search, page, limit
  - Response: {users: User[], total: number, page: number}

- GET /api/v1/users/:userId - Get user by ID
  - Permission: admin or own profile
  - Response: User object

- POST /api/v1/users - Create new user (admin only)
  - Body: {userId, password, name, surname, organization, role}
  - Response: Created user (without password)

- PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId - Update user (admin or own profile)
  - Body: {name?, surname?, organization?, role?, active?}
  - Role and active only editable by admin
  - Response: Updated user

- DELETE /api/v1/users/:userId - Delete user (admin only)
  - Cannot delete yourself
  - Cannot delete last admin
  - Response: 204 No Content

- PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId/password - Change password
  - Body: {currentPassword, newPassword} (if own profile)
  - Body: {newPassword} (if admin changing other user's password)
  - Response: 204 No Content

Implementation notes:
- Reuse existing KrknUser CRD
- Add to internal/api/handlers.go
- Implement proper RBAC (admin vs user permissions)
- Add validation (duplicate email, last admin protection)
- Add tests in internal/api/handlers_test.go
- Update API documentation

This is a blocking requirement for frontend user management feature (tsebastiani-x0f, tsebastiani-74q, tsebastiani-de1, tsebastiani-8be, tsebastiani-33n)
close_reason:
✅ User CRUD API implementation complete

All 6 user CRUD endpoints implemented and integrated:
- ✅ GET /api/v1/users - List users (admin only, with filtering & pagination)
- ✅ GET /api/v1/users/:userId - Get user (admin or self)
- ✅ POST /api/v1/users - Create user (admin only)
- ✅ PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId - Update user (admin or self with restrictions)
- ✅ DELETE /api/v1/users/:userId - Delete user (admin only, safety checks)
- ✅ PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId/password - Change password (admin or self)

Implementation details:
- Created internal/api/user_handlers.go with all 6 handlers
- Created internal/api/user_handlers_test.go with comprehensive tests
- Added user CRUD types to internal/api/types.go
- Integrated UsersRouter into server.go
- Updated Register endpoint (removed TODO, clarified admin-only first registration)
- All handlers have godoc comments and follow existing patterns
- Proper RBAC (admin vs user permissions) implemented
- Safety features: cannot delete self, cannot delete last admin, password validation

Build: ✅ Successful
Routes: ✅ Registered in server.go
Ready for frontend integration

title: Implement PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId/password - Change password
description:
Create endpoint to change user passwords.

Endpoint: PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId/password

Path parameters:
- userId: user's email address

Request body (for changing own password):
{
  "currentPassword": "OldPass123",
  "newPassword": "NewSecurePass456"
}

Request body (admin changing another user's password):
{
  "newPassword": "NewSecurePass456"
}

Response (200):
{
  "message": "Password updated successfully"
}

Authorization:
- Regular users can change only their own password (must provide currentPassword)
- Admin can change any user's password (currentPassword not required)

Validation:
- newPassword must meet password requirements (use auth.ValidatePassword)
- If changing own password, currentPassword must be correct
- newPassword cannot be same as currentPassword

Implementation:
- Add handler in internal/api/user_handlers.go
- Get current user from JWT claims
- Check permissions:
  - If userId == current user -> require currentPassword
  - If admin changing other user -> no currentPassword needed
- Get existing password secret
- Verify currentPassword if provided (use auth.VerifyPassword)
- Hash new password (use auth.HashPassword)
- Update password secret
- Add tests for both self-change and admin-change scenarios

Error responses:
- 401: Not authenticated
- 403: Not authorized to change this user's password
- 404: User not found
- 400: Validation error (weak password, current password incorrect, same password)
- 500: Internal error

Related frontend task: tsebastiani-de1 (password change section)

title: Implement POST /api/v1/users - Create new user
description:
Create endpoint for admins to create new users (replaces Register after first admin).

Endpoint: POST /api/v1/users

Request body:
{
  "userId": "newuser@example.com",
  "password": "SecurePass123",
  "name": "Alice",
  "surname": "Johnson",
  "organization": "Tech Corp",
  "role": "user"  // or "admin"
}

Response (201):
{
  "message": "User created successfully",
  "userId": "newuser@example.com",
  "role": "user"
}

Authorization: Admin only

Validation:
- All fields required except organization (optional)
- userId must be valid email
- userId must not already exist
- Password must meet requirements (use auth.ValidatePassword)
- Role must be "user" or "admin"

Implementation:
- Add handler in internal/api/user_handlers.go
- Reuse logic from Register handler in auth_handlers.go
- Create KrknUser CRD
- Create password secret
- Set Status.Active = true (update status after create)
- Add tests for validation and success cases

Error responses:
- 401: Not authenticated
- 403: Not admin
- 400: Validation error (missing fields, invalid email, weak password)
- 409: User already exists
- 500: Internal error

Related frontend task: tsebastiani-74q

This replaces the TODO in auth_handlers.go:161 - after first admin, use this endpoint instead of /auth/register

title: Implement GET /api/v1/users - List all users
description:
Create endpoint to list all users with filtering and pagination.

Endpoint: GET /api/v1/users

Query parameters:
- role: filter by role (admin/user)
- active: filter by active status (true/false)
- search: search by email, name, or surname
- page: page number (default: 1)
- limit: items per page (default: 50, max: 100)

Response (200):
{
  "users": [
    {
      "userId": "admin@example.com",
      "name": "John",
      "surname": "Doe",
      "organization": "ACME Corp",
      "role": "admin",
      "active": true,
      "created": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z",
      "lastLogin": "2025-01-20T15:30:00Z"
    }
  ],
  "total": 42,
  "page": 1,
  "limit": 50
}

Authorization: Admin only (use auth.IsAdmin)

Implementation:
- Add handler in internal/api/user_handlers.go (new file)
- List KrknUser CRDs from cluster
- Apply filters (role, active, search)
- Implement pagination
- Do NOT include password hashes in response
- Add tests in internal/api/user_handlers_test.go

Error responses:
- 401: Not authenticated
- 403: Not admin
- 500: Internal error listing users

Related frontend task: tsebastiani-x0f

title: Audit all backend endpoints for JWT authentication
description:
Comprehensive audit of all backend API endpoints to ensure proper JWT authentication and authorization.

**Scope:**
Review all HTTP endpoints (REST and WebSocket) to ensure they require authentication except for public endpoints.

**Public Endpoints (no auth required):**
- POST /api/v1/auth/login
- POST /api/v1/auth/register  
- GET /api/v1/auth/is-registered

**Protected Endpoints (must require JWT):**

**Users API:**
- GET /api/v1/users (admin only)
- GET /api/v1/users/{userId}
- POST /api/v1/users (admin only)
- PATCH /api/v1/users/{userId}
- PATCH /api/v1/users/{userId}/password
- DELETE /api/v1/users/{userId} (admin only)

**Providers API:**
- GET /api/v1/providers
- PATCH /api/v1/providers/{name}
- POST /api/v1/provider-config
- GET /api/v1/provider-config/{uuid}
- POST /api/v1/provider-config/{uuid}

**Scenarios API:**
- GET /api/v1/scenarios
- GET /api/v1/scenarios/{name}
- POST /api/v1/scenarios/run
- GET /api/v1/scenarios/run (list)
- GET /api/v1/scenarios/run/{scenarioRunName}
- DELETE /api/v1/scenarios/run/{scenarioRunName}

**Targets API:**
- POST /api/v1/targets
- GET /api/v1/targets/{uuid}
- GET /api/v1/targets
- PUT /api/v1/targets/{uuid}
- DELETE /api/v1/targets/{uuid}

**WebSocket Endpoints:**
- WS /api/v1/scenarios/run/{scenarioRunName}/jobs/{jobId}/logs (see issue tsebastiani-moc)

**Verification Checklist:**
- [ ] All endpoints use JWT middleware except public auth endpoints
- [ ] RBAC enforced (admin-only endpoints check role claim)
- [ ] Token expiration is validated
- [ ] Invalid/expired tokens return 401 Unauthorized
- [ ] Missing tokens return 401 Unauthorized
- [ ] WebSocket endpoints validate token via subprotocol

**Testing:**
- Test each endpoint without token → expect 401
- Test with expired token → expect 401
- Test with invalid signature → expect 401
- Test admin-only endpoints with user role → expect 403
- Test valid token → expect 200/success
close_reason: Completed JWT auth audit. All endpoints secured with authMw.RequireAuth except 3 public auth endpoints. WebSocket auth via subprotocol validated. RBAC needs verification in some handlers. Report: /tmp/jwt_auth_audit_report.md

title: Implement DELETE /api/v1/users/:userId - Delete user
description:
Create endpoint for admins to delete users.

Endpoint: DELETE /api/v1/users/:userId

Path parameters:
- userId: user's email address

Response (204): No Content (success)

Authorization: Admin only

Safety checks:
- Cannot delete yourself (prevent admin lockout)
- Cannot delete the last admin user (prevent system lockout)
- Check if user exists before deletion

Implementation:
- Add handler in internal/api/user_handlers.go
- Get current user from JWT claims (auth.GetClaimsFromContext)
- Check if trying to delete self -> 403 Forbidden
- Check if user exists
- If deleting admin, count remaining admins
  - If this is the last admin -> 403 Forbidden with message
- Delete KrknUser CRD
- Delete associated password secret
- Return 204 No Content on success
- Add tests for all safety checks

Error responses:
- 401: Not authenticated
- 403: Not admin, or cannot delete self, or cannot delete last admin
- 404: User not found
- 500: Internal error

Related frontend task: tsebastiani-8be

title: Test centralized database setup
Verify currentPassword in user self-password-change endpoint
description:
When a user changes their own password via PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId/password, the backend should verify that the 'currentPassword' field is provided and matches the user's current password before allowing the change. This is a security requirement to prevent unauthorized password changes.

Currently, the frontend sends currentPassword when isSelfChange=true, but we need to verify the backend validates it.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Backend validates currentPassword is provided when user changes their own password
- Backend returns 401/403 if currentPassword is missing or incorrect
- Admin password changes (admin changing other user's password) should NOT require currentPassword
- Update API documentation to reflect this validation
close_reason:
✅ CurrentPassword validation already implemented

The backend already validates currentPassword when users change their own password.

Implementation details (user_handlers.go:808-835):
- If user changes own password (isSelf && !isAdmin):
  - Requires currentPassword field (400 error if missing)
  - Verifies currentPassword is correct (400 error if wrong)
  - Validates new password != current password
- If admin changes password (any user):
  - Does NOT require currentPassword
  - Admin can change any password without knowing current

Test coverage:
- TestChangePassword_SelfChange_RequiresCurrentPassword ✅
- TestChangePassword_AdminChange_NoCurrentPassword ✅
- TestChangePassword_Validation ✅
- TestChangePassword_SamePassword_Rejected ✅

Frontend can rely on this validation being enforced by backend.

title: WebSocket authentication via Sec-WebSocket-Protocol subprotocol
description:
**CORREZIONE: Implementazione WebSocket Auth Subprotocol**

Implement secure JWT authentication for WebSocket connections using subprotocol header.

**Current Issue:**
WebSocket connections for log streaming don't support custom HTTP headers like 'Authorization'.

**CORRECTED Solution:**
Use WebSocket Subprotocol with token embedded in protocol name.

**Frontend Implementation (UPDATED):**
```javascript
const protocol = `access_token.${token}`;
const ws = new WebSocket(wsUrl, protocol);
// Browser sends: Sec-WebSocket-Protocol: access_token.<jwt_token>
```

**Backend Requirements (CORRECTED):**

1. **Extract token from subprotocol header:**
   - Read 'Sec-WebSocket-Protocol' header during WebSocket upgrade
   - Protocol format: `access_token.<jwt_token>`
   - Split on first '.' to separate prefix from token
   - Example: `access_token.eyJhbGc...` → prefix=`access_token`, token=`eyJhbGc...`

2. **Validate JWT token:**
   - Use existing JWT validation middleware/function
   - Verify token signature and expiration
   - Extract user claims (userId, role)
   - Return 401 if token invalid/missing

3. **Accept connection:**
   - If token valid: Accept upgrade with `Sec-WebSocket-Protocol: access_token` (just the prefix, NOT the token\!)
   - If token invalid: Reject with HTTP 401 Unauthorized
   - Apply same RBAC rules as REST endpoints

4. **Endpoint affected:**
   - WS /api/v1/scenarios/run/{scenarioRunName}/jobs/{jobId}/logs

**Example Go Implementation (gorilla/websocket):**
```go
// Read the Sec-WebSocket-Protocol header
protocols := r.Header.Get("Sec-WebSocket-Protocol")
if protocols == "" {
    http.Error(w, "Missing Sec-WebSocket-Protocol", 401)
    return
}

// Parse protocol: "access_token.<jwt>"
parts := strings.SplitN(protocols, ".", 2)
if len(parts) \!= 2 || parts[0] \!= "access_token" {
    http.Error(w, "Invalid protocol format", 401)
    return
}

token := parts[1]

// Validate JWT token
claims, err := validateJWT(token)
if err \!= nil {
    http.Error(w, "Invalid token", 401)
    return
}

// Upgrade with ONLY the prefix (not the full token\!)
upgrader := websocket.Upgrader{
    Subprotocols: []string{"access_token"},
}

conn, err := upgrader.Upgrade(w, r, http.Header{
    "Sec-WebSocket-Protocol": []string{"access_token"},
})
if err \!= nil {
    return
}

// Connection established with authenticated user
// Use claims.UserId, claims.Role for authorization
```

**Security Notes:**
- Token never appears in URL, query params, or server logs
- Token transmitted only in WebSocket handshake header
- Browser doesn't log Sec-WebSocket-Protocol values
- Same security level as HTTP Authorization header

**Testing:**
- Test with valid token → expect connection success
- Test with invalid token → expect 401 rejection
- Test with missing token → expect 401 rejection
- Test with expired token → expect 401 rejection
- Verify logs don't contain token values
close_reason: Fixed WebSocket auth to use dot-separated protocol format (access_token.<jwt>) instead of array format. Token extracted via strings.SplitN on Sec-WebSocket-Protocol header. Frontend: new WebSocket(url, 'access_token.' + token)

title: Update Register endpoint - remove TODO and document admin user creation
description:
Update the /api/v1/auth/register endpoint to clarify its purpose and remove TODO.

Current behavior:
- Allows first admin registration without authentication
- Rejects all subsequent registrations with TODO message

Changes needed in internal/api/auth_handlers.go:

1. Remove TODO comment at line 161
2. Update error message to be more helpful:

OLD:
writeJSONError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, ErrorResponse{
    Error:   "authentication_required",
    Message: "Authentication required to register new users (authentication middleware not yet implemented)",
})

NEW:
writeJSONError(w, http.StatusForbidden, ErrorResponse{
    Error:   "registration_closed",
    Message: "Initial admin registration is complete. New users must be created by an admin using POST /api/v1/users",
})

3. Update function godoc to clarify:
// Register handles POST /auth/register
// Registers the FIRST admin user only. After that, use POST /api/v1/users (admin only).

4. Update tests in auth_handlers_test.go:
- Update expected error message
- Change status code from 401 to 403

Rationale:
- 403 Forbidden is more accurate than 401 Unauthorized (they ARE authenticated by being on the network, but FORBIDDEN from using this endpoint)
- Clear message directs admins to the correct endpoint
- Removes TODO that's now resolved by User CRUD API

Dependencies:
- Should be done after tsebastiani-6t8 (POST /api/v1/users) is implemented

Files to modify:
- internal/api/auth_handlers.go
- internal/api/auth_handlers_test.go

title: Delete KrknTargetRequest after scenario run creation
description:
Currently, KrknTargetRequest CRs are not deleted after a scenario run is created. This leads to accumulation of completed target requests.

The KrknTargetRequest should be deleted after the KrknScenarioRun CR is successfully created, as its purpose is complete once the target data is captured in the Secret and used to create the run.

Implementation:
1. After creating KrknScenarioRun CR in PostScenarioRun handler (internal/api/handlers.go:993)
2. Delete the KrknTargetRequest using targetRequestId (req.TargetRequestId)
3. Add error handling - deletion failure should be logged but not fail the scenario run creation
4. Consider adding a grace period or marking as 'consumed' before deletion to allow debugging

The Secret containing managed-clusters data should remain, as it's referenced by the ScenarioRun.

title: Implement PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId - Update user
description:
Create endpoint to update user information.

Endpoint: PATCH /api/v1/users/:userId

Path parameters:
- userId: user's email address

Request body (all fields optional):
{
  "name": "NewName",
  "surname": "NewSurname",
  "organization": "New Org",
  "role": "admin",      // admin only
  "active": false         // admin only
}

Response (200):
{
  "message": "User updated successfully",
  "user": {
    "userId": "user@example.com",
    "name": "NewName",
    "surname": "NewSurname",
    "organization": "New Org",
    "role": "admin",
    "active": false,
    "created": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z",
    "lastLogin": "2025-01-20T15:30:00Z"
  }
}

Authorization:
- Admin can update any user (all fields)
- Regular users can only update their own profile (name, surname, organization only)
- Cannot change userId (email)
- Cannot change role/active unless admin

Validation:
- At least one field must be provided
- If role provided, must be "user" or "admin"
- If active provided, must be boolean

Implementation:
- Add handler in internal/api/user_handlers.go
- Get existing KrknUser CRD
- Check permissions (admin or self)
- Update allowed fields based on role
- Update spec (for name, surname, organization, role)
- Update status separately (for active field)
- Add tests for both admin and regular user updates

Error responses:
- 401: Not authenticated
- 403: Not authorized to update this user or field
- 404: User not found
- 400: Validation error (no fields provided, invalid role)
- 500: Internal error

Related frontend task: tsebastiani-de1

title: Fix EPIPE error on WebSocket log streaming endpoint
description:
**Critical Issue**: WebSocket log streaming endpoint failing with code 1006 (Abnormal Closure)

**Frontend Diagnostic Output:**
```
=== WebSocket Connection Attempt ===
URL: ws://localhost:3000/api/v1/scenarios/run/node-io-hog-fb5e0995/jobs/4beabcc9-a980-4089-b305-5ac66190926d/logs?follow=true
Token available: true
Token length: 385
Protocol format check: access_token. (should be "access_token.") ✅

WebSocket created. ReadyState: 0 (CONNECTING)

=== WebSocket Error Event ===
ReadyState at error: 3 (CLOSED)

=== WebSocket Close Event ===
Close code: 1006
Close reason: (no reason provided)
Was clean: false
Close code meaning: Abnormal closure (connection dropped, no close frame)
```

**Analysis:**
1. Frontend sends correctly formatted WebSocket upgrade request
2. Token present and valid (works for REST APIs)
3. Subprotocol format correct: `access_token.<jwt>`
4. Connection **immediately rejected** by backend
5. Code 1006 = backend closes TCP connection BEFORE completing WebSocket handshake
6. Never reaches `onopen` event (handshake never completes)

**Vite Proxy Configuration (VERIFIED):**
```typescript
proxy: {
  '/api': {
    target: 'http://localhost:8080',
    changeOrigin: true,
    ws: true, // ✅ WebSocket proxying enabled
  }
}
```

**Connection Flow:**
```
Frontend (ws://localhost:3000/api/...) 
  → Vite Proxy (ws: true)
  → Backend (ws://localhost:8080/api/...) 
  → ❌ IMMEDIATE REJECTION (code 1006)
```

**Possible Backend Issues:**
1. ❌ WebSocket endpoint not implemented at all
2. ❌ Endpoint exists but doesn't support Sec-WebSocket-Protocol header
3. ❌ Token parsing crashes (panic/exception)
4. ❌ Backend returns HTTP error (not 101 Switching Protocols) during upgrade
5. ❌ Missing WebSocket route registration

**Backend Debugging Steps:**
1. **Verify endpoint exists:** Check if route `/api/v1/scenarios/run/{name}/jobs/{id}/logs` is registered for WebSocket
2. **Add upgrade logging:**
   ```go
   log.Printf("WebSocket upgrade request received")
   log.Printf("Sec-WebSocket-Protocol: %s", r.Header.Get("Sec-WebSocket-Protocol"))
   ```
3. **Check for errors:** Add error logging BEFORE upgrade attempt
4. **Verify gorilla/websocket upgrader:** Ensure Upgrader.Subprotocols includes "access_token"
5. **Test without auth:** Temporarily accept connection without token validation to verify endpoint works

**Expected Backend Logs (if working):**
```
WebSocket upgrade request received
Sec-WebSocket-Protocol: access_token.eyJhbGc...
Validating JWT token...
Token valid for user: admin@example.com
Accepting WebSocket connection with protocol: access_token
Streaming logs for job: 4beabcc9-a980-4089-b305-5ac66190926d
```

**What's NOT Happening:**
- No upgrade attempt logged (endpoint probably not hit)
- No token validation logged
- No error message sent to client
- Connection simply drops

**Next Steps:**
1. Check backend logs to see if endpoint is being hit
2. Verify WebSocket route is registered in router
3. Add debug logging in upgrade handler
4. Consider temporary fallback to HTTP polling for logs until WebSocket is fixed

**Related Tasks:**
- [tsebastiani-moc] - CLOSED but WebSocket auth still not working
- Frontend has comprehensive diagnostic logging
- Token authentication works for all REST endpoints
close_reason: Fixed in commits 1be7705 and 2fec0f4. WebSocket now properly bypasses HTTP JWT middleware and echoes correct subprotocol. User confirmed working: 'ok abbiamo risolto il websocket\! bravo'
